briefing.title: How Organization Roles Change in the AI Era
briefing.note: AI changed how teams build software. Why it matters: If your team hasn't tried Organisation Roles Change yet, this walkthrough lowers the barrier.

editors_note.headline: This week
editors_note.thesis: This week's signal is clear: business model decisions are moving faster than product roadmaps. Trump moves to ban Anthropic from the US government is the one to keep on your planning radar.
editors_note.hook: The headline drives the week, but the real question is what it means for your vendor assumptions and Q2 plans.
editors_note.spotlight: The lead story is the most likely to change near-term decisions for teams shipping AI products.
editors_note.underhyped: A Complete End-to-End Coding Guide to MLflow Experiment Tracking, Hyperparameter Optimization, Model Evaluation, and Live Model Deployment deserves more attention than it's getting.
editors_note.risk: Vendor and pricing risk is elevated this week — review your dependency list.

lead_story.title: Trump moves to ban Anthropic from the US government
lead_story.what_happened: Developments around Trump are worth a slot in your next ops or leadership review.
lead_story.our_take: Treat this as input for your next sync, not an alarm. Second-order effects matter more than the news itself.
lead_story.what_to_do: Add Trump as a discussion item in your next strategy or planning meeting.

quick_hits.title: How Organization Roles Change in the AI Era
quick_hits.note: AI changed how teams build software. Why it matters: If your team hasn't tried Organisation Roles Change yet, this walkthrough lowers the barrier.

sections.title: Open-source picks
sections.deck: Open-source projects worth a closer look.

articles.title: Show HN: IssueScout – Find open source issues worth contributing to
articles.summary: Open distribution of IssueScout lowers adoption barriers and pressures proprietary alternatives (via Hacker News).
articles.what_happened: IssueScout going open-source lowers barriers for experimentation and custom deployment.
articles.why_it_matters: Open access to IssueScout lowers switching cost and pressures competing proprietary tools.
articles.what_to_do: Review IssueScout issue tracker, last commit date, and license before considering adoption.
articles.our_take: Star velocity tells you what's popular, not what's reliable. Check the issue backlog before the star count.

sections.title: Research worth your time
sections.deck: Research updates with practical product impact.

articles.title: A Complete End-to-End Coding Guide to MLflow Experiment Tracking, Hyperparameter Optimization, Model Evaluation, and Live Model Deployment
articles.summary: Concrete enough to follow along — the End Coding Guide tutorial goes beyond toy examples (via MarkTechPost).
articles.what_happened: New funding around End Coding Guide reflects investor confidence in this part of the stack.
articles.why_it_matters: If End Coding Guide findings replicate, they could change how teams approach related problems.
articles.what_to_do: Compare the End Coding Guide eval setup to yours — check if the benchmark conditions match your workload.
articles.our_take: Use this for direction-setting, not for production bets. Keep it on the watch list.

articles.title: Alibaba Team Open-Sources CoPaw: A High-Performance Personal Agent Workstation for Developers to Scale Multi-Channel AI Workflows and Memory
articles.summary: Open access to Alibaba Team Open gives teams a new self-hosted option to evaluate (via MarkTechPost).
articles.what_happened: Alibaba Team Open released under an open license, giving teams full control over deployment.
articles.why_it_matters: The open release of Alibaba Team Open adds a viable alternative to proprietary options.
articles.what_to_do: Read the Alibaba Team Open abstract. If the problem matches yours, read the method. Otherwise, skip it.
articles.our_take: Don't re-architect on a single paper. Let the results mature, then re-evaluate.

sections.title: Infra
sections.deck: Cloud, scale, and infrastructure that matters to AI builders.

articles.title: OpenClaw, but in containers: Meet NanoClaw
articles.summary: The OpenClaw story has enough signal to track — file it for your next planning round (via The Register).
articles.what_happened: The OpenClaw story is worth a skim — small signals like this tend to add up.
articles.why_it_matters: No immediate action needed, but OpenClaw shifts the background assumptions a bit.
articles.what_to_do: Add OpenClaw to your weekly review and decide on a next step.
articles.our_take: Watch closely rather than act now — the picture will get clearer soon.

articles.title: SaaS-pocalypse chatter is doomster pr0n. It would be nice if enterprise IT were boring again
articles.summary: If SaaS pans out, it could change how teams approach their current stack (via The Register).
articles.what_happened: Not urgent, but SaaS is the kind of thing that shows up again in a month.
articles.why_it_matters: SaaS sounds minor now, but it could change your shortlist by next quarter.
articles.what_to_do: Review SaaS and decide on one concrete follow-up action by end of week.
articles.our_take: Don't let FOMO drive architecture. Validate on your own terms before changing course.

sections.title: What changed in industry

articles.title: The trap Anthropic built for itself
articles.summary: If Anthropic pans out, it could change how teams approach their current stack (via TechCrunch AI).
articles.what_happened: Anthropic in the news; could affect pricing, roadmaps, or compliance this quarter — flag it.
articles.why_it_matters: Industry moves around Anthropic tend to show up in vendor and budget conversations; get ahead of it.
articles.what_to_do: Flag Anthropic for your leadership team — it may shift near-term priorities.
articles.our_take: The signal is real; the timing matters more than the headline. Use it as a planning input (TechCrunch AI).

articles.title: OpenAI and Amazon announce strategic partnership
articles.summary: The OpenAI announcement carries implications for budgets and vendor relationships (via OpenAI Blog).
articles.what_happened: OpenAI in the news; could affect pricing, roadmaps, or compliance this quarter — flag it.
articles.why_it_matters: The OpenAI move could shift the math for your next planning cycle; flag it in leadership review.
articles.what_to_do: Monitor OpenAI for follow-on product or pricing changes over the next month.
articles.our_take: The market is sending a signal. Teams that read it early get a head start.

learn_this_week.title: ChatGPT Prompt Engineering for Developers
learn_this_week.note: Co-created with OpenAI. Learn prompt patterns that work in production, not just demos. Free during platform beta.

prompt_of_the_week.text: Summarize this research paper in 3 bullet points: what they did, what they found, and what I should try in my own work.
prompt_of_the_week.why: The third bullet ('what I should try') is the key. It forces the model past summary into application — turning reading time into actionable next steps for your own projects.
prompt_of_the_week.tip: Paste the abstract and conclusion sections for a quick summary.
